summary: Ukrainian-born French artist and fashion designer. Co-founder of Orphism with husband Robert Delaunay. Known for illustrated artists' books, "simultanist" clothing and dress-poems, and costume/set design. Born Sarah Stern.

works.name: Prose of the Trans-Siberian and of Little Jehanne of France (1913, with Blaise Cendrars)
works.description: Long-format illustrated accordion book of Blaise Cendrars's multicolored poetry alongside paintings of a journey on the Trans-Siberian Railway to France, represented by the Eiffel Tower. Called the first "simultaneous book." Enough copies were published to stack to the height of the Eiffel Tower.

clues.clue: Illustrated Blaise Cendrars's Prose of the Trans-Siberian; first 'simultaneous book' with floods of color swirling across pages

created the first 'simultaneous book,' in which floods of color swirl across the pages

clues.clue: Published enough copies that they could be stacked to reach the height of a certain building (the Eiffel Tower)
examples: purposely published enough copies of her long-format illustration of a Blaise Cendrars poem that they could be stacked to reach the height of a certain building

works.name: Fashion Design / Dress-Poems / Simultanist Clothing
works.description: Applied simultaneism (simultaneous contrasting colors) to fashion. Created 'dress-poems' embroidered with avant-garde poetry by Huidobro and Tzara. Launched a boutique selling simultanist clothing.

clues.clue: Created 'dress-poems' — dresses embroidered with avant-garde poems by Vicente Huidobro and Tristan Tzara
examples: created numerous sketches of clothing featuring text by Vicente Huidobro and Tristan Tzara called 'dress-poems'

concentric circles and zig-zags to emphasize the wearer's movements in a production of Cleopatra

Born as Sarah Stern (Ukrainian-born French artist)

Ukrainian-born French artist whose husband Robert created Simultaneous Contrasts

comprehensive_summary:
Sonia Delaunay (1885-1979), born Sarah Stern in Ukraine, was a French artist and fashion designer who co-founded the art movement Orphism with her husband Robert Delaunay. In quizbowl, she is most frequently identified by her illustrated artists' book collaboration with Blaise Cendrars, Prose of the Trans-Siberian and of Little Jehanne of France (1913), described as the first "simultaneous book" with floods of color swirling across pages. She published enough copies that they could be stacked to reach the height of the Eiffel Tower.

She applied the theory of simultaneisme (simultaneous contrasting colors) to fashion, creating "dress-poems" embroidered with avant-garde poetry by Vicente Huidobro and Tristan Tzara. She designed ballet costumes for Diaghilev's production of Cleopatre, placing a rainbow on the waist and using concentric circles and zig-zags to emphasize movement. She also designed cardboard trapezoid costumes for Tristan Tzara's 1923 Dada play The Gas Heart, in which actors could not escape a riot due to the restrictive costumes. Her other works include the painting Coccinelle, featuring semicircles and arches of varying colors.
recursive_suggestions: Robert Delaunay — husband and co-founder of Orphism
